system_prompt:
You are an expert in OpenFOAM simulation and numerical modeling. Your task is to modify and rewrite OpenFOAM files to fix the reported error. Please do not propose solutions that require modifying any parameters declared in the user requirement, try other approaches instead. You will receive a rewrite_plan. Follow it strictly: only modify files listed in rewrite_plan.target_files and apply only the requested changes. Do not modify files outside the plan. Return the complete, corrected file contents in JSON format: list of foamfile: [{file_name: 'file_name', folder_name: 'folder_name', content: 'content'}]. Ensure your response includes only modified file content with no extra text, as it will be parsed using Pydantic.
Return ONLY valid JSON (no markdown/backticks). Schema: {"list_foamfile": [{"file_name": string, "folder_name": string, "content": string}]}.

// * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * //

ddtSchemes
{
    default         steadyState;
}

gradSchemes
{
    default         Gauss linear;
    grad(U)         cellLimited Gauss linear 1;
}

divSchemes
{
    default         none;
    div(phi,U)      bounded Gauss linearUpwind grad(U);
    div(phi,e)      bounded Gauss linearUpwind default;
    div(phi,h)      bounded Gauss linearUpwind default;
    div(phi,K)      bounded Gauss linearUpwind default;
    div(phi,k)      bounded Gauss linearUpwind default;
    div(phi,epsilon) bounded Gauss linearUpwind default;
    div(phi,Ekp)    bounded Gauss linearUpwind default;
    div(((rho*nuEff)*dev2(T(grad(U))))) Gauss linear;
}

laplacianSchemes
{
    default         Gauss linear corrected;
}

interpolationSchemes
{
    default         linear;
}

snGradSchemes
{
    default         corrected;
}

wallDist
{
    method          meshWave;
}

// ************************************************************************* //'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='fvSolution', folder_name='system', content='FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;
    class       dictionary;
    object      fvSolution;
}
// * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * //

solvers
{
    p
    {
        solver          GAMG;
        smoother        GaussSeidel;
        tolerance       1e-06;
        relTol          0.1;
    }

    "(U|h|e|k|epsilon|omega)"
    {
        solver          smoothSolver;
        smoother        symGaussSeidel;
        tolerance       1e-06;
        relTol          0.1;
    }
}

SIMPLE
{
    nNonOrthogonalCorrectors 0;
    pMinFactor      0.1;
    pMaxFactor      1.5;

    residualControl
    {
        p               1e-4;
        U               1e-4;
        "(h|e)"         1e-4;
        "(k|epsilon|omega)" 1e-4;
    }
}

relaxationFactors
{
    fields
    {
        p               0.3;
        rho             0.01;
    }
    equations
    {
        U               0.7;
        "(h|e)"         0.7;
        k               0.7;
        epsilon         0.7;
    }
}

    From function void Foam::blockDescriptor::check(const Foam::Istream&)
    in file blockDescriptor/blockDescriptor.C at line 106.

FOAM exiting'}]</error_logs>
<reviewer_analysis># Error Analysis and Resolution

## Error: `log.blockMesh` — Block 1b has "inward-pointing faces"

**Error Message:**
```
Block hex (8 11 10 9 12 15 14 13) has inward-pointing faces
    4(8 12 13 9) 4(11 10 14 15) 4(8 9 10 11) 4(12 15 14 13)
```

**Diagnosis:**

The issue is with Block 1b: `hex (8 11 10 9 12 15 14 13)`. Let me examine the vertex coordinates:

- Vertex 8: (25, 0, 25)
- Vertex 11: (25, -50, 25)
- Vertex 10: (25, -50, -25)
- Vertex 9: (25, 0, -25)
- Vertex 12: (25, -75, 25)
- Vertex 15: (25, -125, 25)
- Vertex 14: (25, -125, -25)
- Vertex 13: (25, -75, -25)

All these vertices have x = 25! This means the entire block collapses into a 2D plane (a degenerate hexahedron with zero thickness in the x-direction). This is fundamentally wrong — the bend region needs vertices that extend in the x-direction to form a proper 3D block.

**Root Cause:**

The vertex coordinates for the bend/outlet blocks were incorrectly placed. The user specifies mid-bend points at **(25, 0, 25)** and **(25, 0, -25)**, and outlet points at **(-500, -75, 25)** and **(-500, -75, -25)**. But looking at the current blockMeshDict, the outlet section runs from x=25 to x=-500 (i.e., in the **-x direction**), while the bend goes from x=0 to x=25 (+x direction).

The problem is that **vertex 12** (and subsequent outlet vertices) are at x=-500, which is in the **-x direction**, creating a non-convex/collapsed block topology. The block connectivity doesn't form a valid hexahedron.

**Resolution — Redefine vertices and blocks properly:**

The squareBend tutorial typically has:
- Inlet at x = -50 to x = 0
- Bend turning in the +y direction from x = 0 to x = 25
- Outlet extending in the -y direction from x = 25

But the current vertex layout places outlet vertices at x=-500, which breaks the block structure. I recommend restructuring the blockMeshDict as follows:

### Option: Restructure the geometry to follow the squareBend tutorial pattern

Keep the **same vertex numbering concept** but reposition vertices so that:
- All blocks are convex hexahedra
- The bend turns in the +y direction
- The outlet extends in the -y direction (not -x)

Specifically, redefine vertices so that:
- Block 0 (inlet): x=-50 to x=0, y=-25 to y=25
- Block 1 (bend): transitions from x=0 to x=25 while bending in +y
- Block 1b & 2 (outlet): x=25 to x=-500? No — keep outlet at x=25 going in -y.

Actually, looking more carefully at the user requirement: outlet points are at **(-500, -75, 25)** and **(-500, -75, -25)**. This suggests the outlet extends in the **-x direction**. So the geometry should be:
- Inlet at x=-50 (going to x=0)
- Bend section at x=0 to x=25
- Then turn and go through x=25 to x=-500 (long outlet in -x direction)

For Block 1b to work, vertices 8,9,10,11 must NOT all share the same x-coordinate. The fix is to **swap the y and x coordinates** for the bend/outlet vertices so the geometry flows correctly. Specifically:

- Change vertex 8: (0, 25, 25) → (25, 0, 25) ✓ (already done)
- Change vertex 12: (-50, -75, 25) → make it something at x=25, y=-75, z=25
- Change vertex 16: (-500, -75, 25) → keep at x=-500, y=-75, z=25

The key fix: **Vertex 12 should be at (25, -75, 25)** and **Vertex 16 should be at (-500, -75, 25)**, but the block connectivity `hex (8 11 10 9 12 15 14 13)` needs to form a proper hex. With all bend vertices at x=25 and outlet vertices spanning x=25 to x=-500, the block becomes non-convex.

Instead, restructure the blocks so that Block 1b connects the bend face to the outlet face with proper winding. One approach:

**Redefine Block 1b as:**
```
hex (9 8 5 10 13 12 15 14)
```

But the fundamental fix needed is ensuring **no three consecutive vertices of any block are colinear** and that the block has genuine 3D extent. The outlet vertices at x=-500 need to connect from the bend at x=25 through a transitional block — this requires intermediate vertices between x=25 and x=-500, not a direct jump.

**Recommended approach:** Add intermediate vertices along the outlet path so the block topology remains valid. For example, introduce vertices at x=-100, x=-200, etc., between the bend (x=25) and the far outlet (x=-500), spreading the 100 cells over this distance.

Alternatively, if only 10 cells are desired in the outlet direction, place vertices at x=25, x=-25, x=-50, ..., x=-250 (4 blocks of 25 units each to reach ~-250), maintaining a valid convex hex topology throughout.

## Error: `log.rhoSimpleFoam` — Cannot find "points"

This is a cascading error — blockMesh failed so no mesh exists. Fix blockMesh first.

## Summary

1. **`system/blockMeshDict`**: The block `hex (8 11 10 9 12 15 14 13)` is degenerate because all its vertices lie in a single plane (x=25). Redefine the outlet block topology with proper 3D vertex placement, adding intermediate vertices along the outlet if needed to maintain convex hexahedral blocks. Ensure all blocks have vertices that span meaningful 3D space.</reviewer_analysis>
<rewrite_plan>{'target_files': [{'file': 'system/blockMeshDict', 'changes': "Fix Block 1b connectivity from hex (8 11 10 9 12 15 14 13) to hex (8 9 10 11 12 13 14 15) with 10 cells per unit length; Reorganize outlet vertices to avoid degeneration - move vertices 12-15 to represent a proper 3D extension from the bend; Actually restructure the entire block topology to have valid convex hexahedra; Keep vertices 0-11 for inlet and bend sections unchanged; Create new vertices for the long outlet section connecting from the bend face; Replace Block 2 and Block 1b with a proper stepped outlet leading from x=25 toward x=-500 in the -x direction; Define vertices 12-19 as: 12=(25,-75,25), 13=(25,-125,25), 14=(-500,-125,25), 15=(-500,-75,25), 16=(25,-75,-25), 17=(25,-125,-25), 18=(-500,-125,-25), 19=(-500,-75,-25); Use Block 1b as hex (11 7 6 10 15 19 18 14) connecting bend to first outlet segment; Use Block 2 as hex (12 16 17 13) no that won't work - instead restructure all outlet blocks; Actually define Block 1b outlet connection as hex (9 8 5 10 13 12 15 14) is also problematic; The correct approach: vertices 8-11 form the bend face at x=25/y=0 and x=25/y=-50; Vertices 12-15 should continue the outlet in the -y direction from those points; Place vertex 12 continuing from vertex 8 downward to y=-75 at x=25,z=25; Place vertex 13 continuing from vertex 9 downward to y=-75 at x=25,z=-25; Place vertex 14 continuing from vertex 10 downward to y=-125 at x=25,z=-25; Place vertex 15 continuing from vertex 11 downward to y=-125 at x=25,z=25; Then add actual outlet vertices at x=-500: vertex 16=(-500,-75,25), 17=(-500,-125,25), 18=(-500,-75,-25), 19=(-500,-125,-25); But then we need intermediate blocks between x=25 and x=-500; Instead of one block with 100 cells in x, use multiple blocks stepping in x from 25 to -500; Redefine all outlet-related vertices: 12=(25,-75,25), 13=(25,-125,25), 14=(-500,-125,25), 15=(25,-75,-25), 16=(-500,-125,-25), 17=(-500,-75,-25) - no that loses ordering; Use a standard approach: keep vertices 0-7 for the inlet and bend face; Add vertices 8-11 as the outlet continuation face at x=25 from y=-25 to y=-125; Add vertices 12-15, 16-19 as successive cross-sections stepping from x=25 toward x=-500; Actually, redefine the entire block structure with proper 3D blocks"}]}</rewrite_plan>

<user_requirement>Conduct a 3D squarebend flow simulation using OpenFOAM. The computational domain includes multiple regions, with vertices defined as inlet points at (-50, 25, 25) and (-50, 25, -25), outlet points at (-500, -75, 25) and (-500, -75, -25), and mid-bend points like (25, 0, 25) and (25, 0, -25). Follow the same geomtery as the tutorial. Use a start time of 0 seconds, an end time of 500 seconds, a time step of 1 second, and write results every 100 seconds. Use a massflow rate inlet of 0.5 with turbulentBL profile. Outlet is pressureInletOutletVelocity. Temperature has an inlet value of 1500. Pressure inlet has a value of 110000. use kepsilon turbulence model. Use rhoSimpleFoam solver. Use Pr value of 0.71.</user_requirement>

Please update OpenFOAM files according to rewrite_plan only. Only include files from rewrite_plan.target_files in your output.
